ogTitle: Shailer Park vs Shannonvale: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Shailer Park and Shannonvale.
heading: Shailer Park vs Shannonvale — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Shailer Park, Queensland and Shannonvale, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Shannonvale has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Shailer Park (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Shailer Park or Shannonvale safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Shailer Park has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Shannonvale scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Shailer Park scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. Shannonvale scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Shailer Park or Shannonvale?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Shannonvale has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
